title: bromus hordeaceus var. pacensis
intro: Bromus hordeaceus var. pacensis, also known as Pacific brome or cheatgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Bromus hordeaceus var. pacensis include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs and sore throats, and providing relief from colds and flu. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
